extraction.one_line_summary: VAN AERT Wout won after being completely isolated and forced to chase solo in the final 20km, still holding off LAURANCE and KAMP in the sprint.
tactical_patterns: Tratnik attacked solo entering the finishing circuit when the chase group swelled to 15 riders, recognizing numbers killed breakaway viability.
Van Aert bridged alone to chase groups while Jumbo-Visma provided zero support, signaling team's willingness to sacrifice him as domestique earlier.
Lotto-Soudal executed full leadout for De Lie chasing UCI points for World Tour survival, prioritizing relegation battle over podium.
Northwestern roads near Plouay caused multiple crashes as peloton accelerated, making positioning before this narrow sector critical for contenders.

rider_signals.VAN AERT Wout: positive: won despite total isolation and solo chasing duty in final 20km, demonstrating Worlds form but questionable team support
rider_signals.LAURANCE Axel: positive: 21-year-old nearly upset Van Aert in sprint for B&B Hotels, signals emerging threat in reduced bunch sprints
rider_signals.TRATNIK Jan: positive: correctly read that 15-rider chase group was tactically dead, attacked solo entering finishing circuit with climbing power
rider_signals.DE LIE Arnaud: neutral: had full Lotto leadout but finished fourth, suggesting not yet elite finisher despite team investment
rider_signals.COSNEFROY Benoît: negative: defending champion relegated to leadout duty for Naesen, AG2R prioritizing different leader than previous year
